access_notes: The public WordPress writings, about, and training pages are readable without a gate. The robots path redirects to HTML rather than supplying valid directives, and the site asserts ordinary copyright. Several pieces are reposted interviews with layered magazine/publication history, so only a concise, quotation-light, fully attributed factual and testimonial digest is appropriate.

representative_pages.note: Osada Steve's July 2005 interview with Akechi Denki records first-person views on communication, practice, terminology, influences, hojojutsu research, stage work, and muga.
A 2006 Nureki Chimuo interview/observational article contains direct comments about teaching, technique and heart, body adaptation, and public presentation, surrounded by strong interviewer framing.
Interview with Yukimura Haruki discusses camera work, terminology, Minomura Ko, body-specific adaptation, timing, and aesthetics as first-person testimony.
First-party description of Osada-ryu influences, grade progression, and competency criteria including flow, communication, efficiency, and aesthetics.

decision_reason: Rare direct testimony from Akechi, Nureki, and Yukimura plus Osada-ryu's own lineage and terminology is valuable when evidence layers and rights limits remain explicit.
recommended_crawl_scope: Allowlist the writings index, Akechi Denki, Nureki Chimuo, Yukimura Haruki, Osada Eikichi, Oda Hisashi, Osada Steve, terminology essays, about, and training pages. Produce an original, quotation-light digest that records speaker, interviewer/author, interview or repost date, original publication when stated, Japanese name/term, first-person status, and uncertainty. Keep direct testimony separate from interviewer narration, promotion, recollection, and later synthesis; cross-check dates, lineage, hojojutsu, linguistic, anatomy, and safety claims against stronger sources and preserve disagreements. Exclude images, comments, dojo listings, student promotion, and extended prose; do not turn historical gender norms, injury claims, or master/disciple rhetoric into universal consent or technique rules.

source_name: Hajime Kinoko Official Profile and Rope History
access_notes: The official English profile is publicly readable and the host's robots permit the page. It is ordinary copyrighted first-party biography, statement, and career chronology mixed with extensive images, promotion, tours, and media credits; only a narrow attributed factual digest should be retained.

representative_pages.note: First-party statement, declared rope lineage through Yumeko, Akechi Denki, Kanna, Yukimura Haruki, Norio Sugiura and others, plus a dated career and publication chronology.

decision_reason: The profile is the clearest first-party record of Kinoko's declared teachers, influences, practice development, artistic statement, school founding, and selected dated work.
recommended_crawl_scope: Capture a concise attributed digest of the official profile's first-person artistic statement; declared learning from or relationships with Yumeko, Teru, Akechi Denki, Kanna, Yukimura Haruki, Chiharu Karino, Norio Sugiura, and other explicitly named influences; formation of Ichinawa-kai; and selected dated milestones that explain the development of his rope, photography, teaching, performance, or installation practice. Preserve Japanese terms such as shisho and onshi as the profile uses them and label all lineage as self-described. Exclude images, embedded video, galleries, current tours/events, exhaustive celebrity/commercial credits, purchase links, promotional superlatives, and volatile schedules; do not infer technique or safety from visual work.

source_name: Go Arisue Official Profile
access_notes: The newly relaunched official Japanese site is robots-allowed and its substantive biography is public. The site describes itself as a provisional January 2026 reopening and is mostly images, commerce, events, and forthcoming paid material, so only the stable biography has corpus value.

representative_pages.note: Official Japanese biography summarizes Arisue's early-1980s debut, magazine and film work, technical supervision, workshops, writing, photography, stage work, and bakutaku practice.
Official home page dates the provisional relaunch to January 1, 2026 and separates the biography from forthcoming paid content and promotional material.

decision_reason: Although small, the official biography supplies a first-party Japanese chronology and role descriptions that can correct or qualify secondary profiles.
recommended_crawl_scope: Capture only the stable Japanese biography and a careful, source-labeled English factual digest of its dates, roles, named films/directors, technical-supervision work, workshops, instructional publications, writing, photography, stage practice, and bakutaku description. Record the site's January 2026 provisional status and distinguish biography claims from independently verified credits. Exclude images, galleries, event listings, work inquiries, social links, sales, forthcoming paid note content, exhaustive publicity, and external storefronts; do not translate promotional superlatives as neutral facts.

source_name: The Work and Life of Iida Toyokazu / Nureki Chimuo
access_notes: The institutional repository record and 40-page version of record are public and robots-allowed, but the item displays no reuse license. Ritsumeikan's site policy prohibits duplication, modification, or distribution without prior permission. The work's unusually strong source criticism does not override those terms.

representative_pages.note: Durable repository metadata for Azumi Kawahara's 2021 study, DOI 10.34382/0002003261, pages 14-53 of Ritsumeikan Bungaku 674; its license field is blank.
Official policy says duplication, modification, and distribution require the copyright holder's prior permission except where copyright law otherwise permits.

decision_reason: This is the strongest source-critical Nureki biography discovered, but a transformed translation or corpus copy requires explicit permission from the relevant rightsholder.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not download, translate, transform, or queue the article body without written permission for derivative corpus and training use from the author and/or publisher. Retain only repository metadata, DOI, citation, and the permission requirement. If cleared, preserve the study's chronology, publication and pseudonym evidence, five-interview dates, explicit loss of source audio, reconstructed-transcript caveat, distinction between Nureki's testimony and corroborated fact, bibliography, and the author's critique of Nureki's own historical account; do not flatten contested recollection into settled lineage.

source_name: Bibliographic Information and Complete Contents of Early Kitan Club
access_notes: The J-STAGE metadata page for Azumi Kawahara's 2025 85-page source introduction is public. It is marked free-to-read, not Creative Commons, and displays copyright © 2025 Antitled Society. J-STAGE robots disallow generic access to *_pdf routes, so the PDF is not an authorized capture endpoint.

representative_pages.note: Official metadata gives author, article type, pages 273-357, dates, DOI 10.57269/antitled.4.0_273, free-reading status, and ordinary copyright.
Current platform directives disallow generic crawler access to *_pdf endpoints even though article metadata HTML is readable.

decision_reason: The bibliography could anchor early Kitan Club publication history, but neither the article rights nor the PDF endpoint permit the intended corpus capture.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not access the J-STAGE *_pdf route, mirror, translate, or queue the article without both a robots-permitted copy and written permission for derivative corpus/training use from the author or Antitled Society. Retain only the public citation, DOI, author, dates, article type, page range, and permission requirement. If cleared, extract source-critical issue chronology, bibliographic fields, contents indexing, editorial/person relationships, uncertainty, and methodological commentary; exclude scans and any underlying magazine expression not separately licensed.

source_name: Full Circle Kink Bondage for Beginners Handout
access_notes: Public indexing identifies a formerly official beginner handout and a permissive CC BY-SA 4.0 notice, but the exact official PDF currently returns 404 and the host home page is only a DreamHost placeholder. The body and license could not be revalidated from the actual source, so no mirror or cached copy was used.

representative_pages.note: Exact formerly indexed official asset now returns 404; search snippets are not treated as source content or current licensing proof.
Current official domain is a generic site-placeholder page rather than the former educational site.

decision_reason: The apparently reusable guide is promising, but neither the full body nor its license is currently verifiable at the rightsholder's official endpoint.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not queue a search-cache, archive, or third-party copy. Reconsider only if the rightsholder restores the official handout or supplies an authorized copy with the complete CC BY-SA 4.0 attribution and version history. If restored, inventory its actual modules before extraction, preserve author/version/license and share-alike obligations, review every safety claim against current evidence, and capture text-supported knots, negotiation, nerve-risk, rope care, coiling, and pedagogy without inferring from images.

source_name: Devil Mask Studio Series Curriculum
access_notes: The current Studio Series 201, 301, and 401 pages are public and robots-allowed. They provide unusually detailed curriculum maps and prerequisites, but not full lessons, and remain ordinary copyrighted course descriptions embedded in a commercial studio site.

representative_pages.note: 201 map covers body-adapted tengu, agura, futomomo, hip, body, arms-front, and chest harness study after single-column competence.
301 map covers uplines/attachments/lockoffs, partials, force vectors and line tension, nawajiri leverage, ground-testing suspendable harnesses, go/no-go decisions, and body bamboo.
401 map identifies prerequisites and advanced study of upright, hashira, inversion, side, torsion, dynamic transition, in-air communication, and upline-tension topics.

decision_reason: The curriculum maps add concrete prerequisite and progression structure around harness adaptation, uplines, testing, mechanics, transitions, and problem solving without pretending to be standalone tutorials.
recommended_crawl_scope: Allowlist only the substantive Studio Series 102, 201, 301, and 401 curriculum pages that are publicly accessible. Create a concise attributed pedagogy map preserving track order, prerequisites, intended competency, named technical topics, harness/body adaptation, uplines and lockoffs, line tension and force vectors, ground testing and go/no-go reasoning, nawajiri redistribution, bamboo pressure distribution, hashira, inversions, transitions, communication, and problem-solving emphasis. Treat descriptions as the studio's curriculum, not validated instructions; do not invent construction steps, certify any harness or hardpoint, or infer from images. Exclude class dates, tickets, membership, shop, services, staff promotion, venue details, and event listings.

source_name: Rope Study Responsible Progression
access_notes: The current home and about pages are readable through an ordinary browser route and identify named educators and a 101-401 progression. The site warns that a complete overhaul begun May 1, 2026 may leave content missing or malformed; direct command-line requests, including robots and sitemap, currently return a ModSecurity 406. A capture worker must not bypass any challenge and must snapshot only pages available through normal public browsing.

representative_pages.note: Current public overview defines responsible progression, risk awareness, tying well, partnered learning, and the 101/201/301/401 sequence from orientation through full suspension.
Named first-party educators describe the site's history, deliberate-practice pedagogy, informed consent, partnership, stewardship, teaching responsibility, humility, and lineage acknowledgment.

decision_reason: The currently visible pages add a rare explicit competency progression and two-sided pedagogy, provided the unstable rebuild and access boundary are preserved.
recommended_crawl_scope: On a fresh access-policy check, capture only the current public home and about pages, plus any restored topic/progression page reachable through ordinary public navigation without a 406, challenge, login, or workaround. Snapshot retrieval date and page status because the May 2026 rebuild is incomplete. Preserve the 101-401 prerequisites and conceptual progression, distinctions among practice/performance/play, deliberate practice, risk decisions, top/bottom partnership, stewardship, teaching limits, humility, and lineage acknowledgment as Rope Study's pedagogy. Exclude contact/social links, missing-page inference, obsolete cached lessons, and any claim that this outline itself teaches suspension technique or certifies competence.

source_name: RebornRopes Technical Rope and Knot Guides
discovered_from: rope construction care and friction gap review
access_notes: Shopify robots explicitly allow public pages and the store publishes agent-facing read-only browsing instructions. The technical library is unusually broad, but it is ordinary copyrighted vendor content with heavy commerce and no visible citations. Review found disputed knot equivalences, unsafe single-column framing, and an extreme unsupported JBO inhalation claim, so it is not ready for corpus use.

representative_pages.note: Detailed maker explanation of yarn, ply, strands, twisted versus braided construction, lay direction, stiffness, abrasion, and high-stranding, with several uncited strength claims.
First-party maintenance methods and a measured rope-thinning demonstration are mixed with fire, oil, washing, chemical, and health claims requiring specialist review.
Large knot/friction terminology index contains useful names but also questionable equivalences and safety classifications.
Index links individual X, ram's-head, L, right-angle, half-hitch, nodome, girth, reverse-tension, and other friction tutorials, many of which rely on images.

decision_reason: The maker library could fill materials and friction gaps, but unresolved technical and health inaccuracies make direct ingestion more likely to degrade than improve the dataset.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not queue until a knowledgeable rope educator and cordage specialist adjudicate the public technical pages against stronger sources. If cleared, allowlist only text-supported construction, yarn/ply/strand/lay, material-specific care, inspection, retirement, finishing, coiling, joining, and knot/friction terminology that survives review; label maker observations and measured demonstrations as such. Explicitly exclude or correct unsupported JBO respiratory-collapse/death language, unsafe reef/granny single-column framing, disputed Somerville/Carrick and lark's-head/girth equivalences, uncited strength/load claims, and any instruction visible only in images. Exclude every product, price, cart, account, review, shipping, jewelry, gallery, and promotion page.

source_name: German Wikibooks Shibari Manual
access_notes: Wikimedia Commons provides a 60-page February 2012 snapshot under CC BY-SA 3.0 and the underlying German Wikibook remains licensed and public. Permissive licensing does not resolve quality: the source says it is 70% complete, has no current maintainer, and its main page was last edited in 2015.

representative_pages.note: File record supplies authorship/history and CC BY-SA 3.0 terms for the 2012 60-page PDF snapshot.
Current project page says the book is about 70% complete, has no present sponsor, and was last edited in September 2015.
Actual licensed manual contains dated history, safety, material, knot, pattern, glossary, and practitioner sections, including multiple unsafe or unsupported recommendations.

decision_reason: The manual's age, incompleteness, weak sourcing, gendered framing, unsupported origin story, and hazardous equipment, rope-processing, and pattern advice outweigh the convenience of its license.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not queue or train on this manual. Its CC BY-SA status may permit reuse, but quality review found outdated and unsafe material including 90°C washing, abrasive break-in, gun oil, improvised quick-release hardware guidance, unsupported safety-factor rules, neck-adjacent pattern construction, rigid gender assumptions, and an overly certain hojojutsu-origin narrative. Retain only the citation, license, version date, and rejection rationale in discovery; prefer current named educators and source-critical histories.

source_name: Wood Handbook: Wood as an Engineering Material, 2021
discovered_from: indoor hardpoint and structural-wood gap review
access_notes: GovInfo provides a robots-permitted official package, 543-page PDF, and MODS metadata for USDA Forest Service General Technical Report FPL-GTR-282. Federal-authored text is public-domain-eligible under 17 USC 105, but credited standards, figures, tables, photographs, and other third-party matter require item-level rights review.

representative_pages.note: Official package identity and downloadable formats for the 2021 USDA Forest Service engineering handbook.
Official handbook covers wood structure, moisture, mechanical properties, stress grades, fastenings, connectors, structural analysis, engineered wood, deterioration, and building applications.
Official machine-readable provenance and publication metadata for FPL-GTR-282.

decision_reason: This authoritative engineering reference fills the indoor structural-material gap with assumptions and failure variables absent from bondage sites, while requiring an explicit non-certification boundary.
recommended_crawl_scope: Capture official MODS and only relevant federal text on wood structure, species and grade variability, moisture and duration effects, mechanical properties, fasteners and connector joints, edge and spacing variables, engineered wood, deterioration, inspection, and structural-analysis assumptions. Preserve chapter/page, units, test context, load direction and duration, species/grade/moisture conditions, uncertainty, and every warning. Audit and omit third-party credited expression unless separately cleared. This source does not certify any ceiling, beam, joist, connector, tree, hardpoint, bondage system, or human-suspension use; site-specific assessment belongs to a qualified structural engineer, and handbook values must never be converted into a do-it-yourself capacity guarantee.

source_name: How safe is BDSM? A literature review on fatal outcome in BDSM play
discovered_from: rope and BDSM fatality evidence search
access_notes: The robots-permitted EMBL-EBI Europe PMC API exposes complete CC BY 4.0 JATS binding PMCID PMC8813685, DOI 10.1007/s00414-021-02674-0, and PMID 34383118. Generic publisher or mirror routes are unnecessary. The review is a small publication-derived forensic case series without a population denominator and cannot estimate incidence.

representative_pages.note: Europe PMC record binding PMCID PMC8813685, DOI 10.1007/s00414-021-02674-0, and PMID 34383118.
Authorized CC BY 4.0 JATS contains methods, 17 publication-derived cases, forensic terminology, recommendations, limitations, and references.

decision_reason: The licensed review adds rare forensic evidence about fatal outcomes and classification boundaries, provided its severe selection limits and questionable inferences remain prominent.
recommended_crawl_scope: Capture only the CC BY 4.0 JATS from the EMBL-EBI Europe PMC API and bind PMCID PMC8813685, DOI 10.1007/s00414-021-02674-0, and PMID 34383118 in the manifest. Preserve search method, inclusion criteria, 17-case publication-derived denominator, terminology, case-source provenance, uncertainty, selection and publication bias, lack of a population denominator, and inability to estimate incidence or causal risk. Distinguish consensual rope bondage from neck compression, erotic asphyxia, self-bondage, hanging, and other mechanisms; source-label the paper's recommendations and questionable correlations rather than treating them as settled. Exclude sensational narrative, identifiable case detail, instructional reconstruction, and unsupported generalization.

source_name: Yukimura-ryu Official Archive
access_notes: The public WordPress archive is robots-allowed and contains signed first-person testimony, a message from Yukimura's son, Osada Steve's recollection, and school curriculum. It declares all rights reserved, so only an original quotation-light attributed digest is appropriate. Its pages conflict on whether Osada became Yukimura's first formal student in 2006 or 2007.

representative_pages.note: First-party school kyū progression and license structure; its 2006 student date conflicts with another archive page.
Osada recollection says he met Yukimura in 2001 and was accepted as a student in 2007.
Keisuke Higure describes a roughly 20-year working relationship with his father as first-person family testimony.
Yukimura interview discusses aesthetics, emotional exchange, terminology, photography, body-specific adaptation, and timing as dated personal views.
School description of newaza concepts and pedagogy, not independently validated technique guidance.

decision_reason: The archive supplies rare direct Yukimura, family, and student testimony plus a named progression, but copyright, recollection, promotion, and internal date conflicts constrain extraction.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create a quotation-light, fully attributed digest of Yukimura Haruki's first-person statements, Keisuke Higure's family testimony, Osada Steve's recollection, named practice concepts, terminology, artistic context, and kyū/license progression. Record speaker, author/interviewer, original and repost dates, publication context, and claim type. Preserve the unresolved 2006/2007 contradiction about Osada's formal-student date rather than reconciling it; cross-check all lineage, historical, linguistic, anatomy, and safety claims. Treat gendered, sexual, philosophical, and master/student statements as dated individual views, not present-day consent norms. Exclude images, video, extended quotations, schedules, fees, promotion, and technique inferred from media.

source_name: Akechi Kanna Official Note Writings
access_notes: The public Note article and profile are robots-allowed and identify first-person views and a claimed succession from Akechi Denki. Note's terms state that creators retain copyright and prohibit reproduction, publication, modification, or adaptation beyond platform use without creator consent; no creator reuse permission was found.

representative_pages.note: March 11, 2026 first-person essay explicitly frames its account of kinbaku, sexuality, interaction, conversation, and care as Kanna's personal view.
Public class article supplies first-party teaching context but is not licensed for reproduction or adaptation.
Official platform terms retain creator copyright and restrict reproduction, publication, modification, and adaptation without consent.

decision_reason: The first-person lineage and practice perspective is valuable, but platform terms make translation or transformed corpus capture inappropriate without direct creator permission.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not copy, translate, transform, adapt, or queue the article bodies without written permission from Akechi Kanna for derivative corpus and training use. Retain only public citation metadata, the canonical URLs, date, self-described lineage lead, and permission requirement. If permission is obtained, create an attributed, quotation-light digest that keeps personal philosophy separate from universal consent, history, or technique claims; exclude class promotion, images, and media.

source_name: Shibaru History of Shibari Series
access_notes: The four signed public articles by Nawakiri Shin (小林縄霧), dated 2015 and 2018, are robots-allowed and ordinary copyrighted. They combine practitioner synthesis, bibliography, images, and historical claims; only an original, quotation-light English factual digest with evidence labels is appropriate.

representative_pages.note: Part 1 discusses hojojutsu as one proposed historical context and requires careful separation of sourced evidence from continuity claims.
Part 2 traces performance and visual motifs through Kabuki as practitioner history synthesis.
Part 3 discusses ukiyo-e and erotic or punitive imagery without establishing direct technique transmission.
Part 4 supplies the strongest postwar magazine, contributor-network, censorship, and technique-development leads, plus a bibliography.

decision_reason: The signed Traditional-Chinese series adds a useful practitioner bridge from prewar visual/performance contexts to the postwar Kitan Club network, provided lineage certainty and safety claims are not inherited.
recommended_crawl_scope: Allowlist only the four exact history-series articles dated 2015 and 2018. Produce an original quotation-light English synthesis attributed to Nawakiri Shin/小林縄霧; record article date, language, claim type, cited source, and uncertainty. Preserve useful leads about hojojutsu, Kabuki, ukiyo-e, ero-guro, Kitan Club contributors, censorship, publishing networks, and post-1950s technique change while explicitly separating visual analogy, practitioner interpretation, secondary scholarship, and corroborated chronology. Exclude images, scans, full translation, historical technique instruction, and extended prose. Quarantine the unsupported kannuki bruising-frequency claim, chest-band slippage claims, and all technique or safety generalizations unless corroborated by stronger evidence.

source_name: Willcat Ropes / Tension Curriculum
access_notes: A long public page by William Desjardins (Willcat) presents a named developing curriculum, prerequisites, assessment, and teaching philosophy. The page is ordinary copyrighted, commercial, explicitly under construction, and includes unsupported history, linguistic, anatomy, and safety assertions.

representative_pages.note: First-party curriculum lists staged fundamentals, named knots/frictions and pattern concepts, partnered skills, lab integration, readiness assessment, and prerequisites before load or suspension work.

decision_reason: The evolving curriculum contributes concrete sequencing, two-sided practice, and assessment structure, but it is not a stable or independently validated tutorial.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create a concise quotation-light pedagogy map attributed to Willcat/Tension: declared educator background and limits, prerequisite and assessment structure, fundamentals, named knots/frictions and pattern concepts, negotiation, aftercare, debrief, tying and bottoming skill development, lab/integration stages, and the progression before uplines or load. Preserve the page's under-construction status and distinguish curriculum labels from validated instructions. Exclude unsupported history and linguistic claims, medical/anatomy guidance, pressure or body-reading claims, absolute safety statements such as appearance implying correctness, construction steps, prices, bookings, class promotion, images, and media-only detail.

source_name: ESINEM Public Rope-Maker Articles
discovered_from: rope lay and handling terminology search
access_notes: Public WordPress article routes are robots-allowed, but the footer states all content is copyright Esinem/Bruce Argue and all rights reserved. This candidate is distinct from the already queued ESINEM-hosted Senses of Shibari dissertation. The articles mix firsthand rope-making observations, tutorial promotion, vendor claims, and unsupported safety analogies.

representative_pages.note: Firsthand maker discussion of tangles, high-stranding, rope lay, strand balance, and handling behavior.
Named mechanics and terminology are embedded in promotion and are not a standalone validated lesson.
Incident interpretation contains vendor bias and unsupported material and sizing recommendations.
High-risk technique discussion makes an explicitly assumed death-triangle analogy and should not be ingested as safety evidence.

decision_reason: A very small core of source-labeled rope-maker observations about lay, strand imbalance, high-stranding, and tangling is novel enough to retain, while the surrounding technique, history, health, load, and sales claims are unsuitable.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create only a quotation-light attributed digest of Bruce Argue's firsthand rope-maker observations about rope lay, strand imbalance, high-stranding, tangling, and handling behavior, cross-checked against stronger cordage references. Label demonstrations and incident interpretations as maker observations, not established general rules. Exclude copied prose and images, product and sales claims, tutorial construction steps, history or translation claims, suspension load and rope-size recommendations, nerve/anatomy claims, the assumed American Death Triangle analogy, gote safety assertions, uncorroborated breakage causation, prices, catalog, reviews, events, and all promotion.

source_name: RopeBite Pittsburgh Class Notes and Starting Guides
access_notes: The public WordPress pages are robots-allowed and ordinary copyrighted. Manual review found sparse original text, video dependence, heavy overlap with stronger queued sources, medication and supplement recommendations for nerve injury, and hazardous heat/flame rope-processing instructions.

representative_pages.note: Beginner class notes largely duplicate stronger named curricula and include uncited health and material claims.
Advises boiling natural rope, gas-flame singeing, and baking treated rope and paper towels in an oven.
The upline page is predominantly externally linked video rather than self-contained text-supported instruction.

decision_reason: Unsupported medical advice, hazardous rope-processing steps, visual dependence, and near-total overlap make this more likely to degrade the corpus than add reliable knowledge.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not queue or train on this site. Explicitly reject its ibuprofen and vitamin B12 nerve-injury advice; instructions to boil rope, singe it over a gas flame, or bake oiled/waxed rope and paper towels in an oven; unsupported claims that dryness causes most rope breaks; and broad uncited material or safety rules. Do not reconstruct externally hosted video tutorials. Retain only the canonical citation and rejection rationale; prefer Crash Restraint, stronger named rope curricula, clinical sources, and authoritative cordage references.

source_name: Rope Bondage the Smart Way
access_notes: The author-hosted 2017 PDF is public and 345 pages. Its rights page says all rights reserved and prohibits reproduction or storage in a retrieval system without prior written permission. The author also describes roughly six years of intermittent learning and warns that opinions are not guaranteed accurate; the body has substantial overlap and needs full safety review.

representative_pages.note: Official 345-page manual; page 3 prohibits reproduction and retrieval-system storage without permission, and page 7 states author-experience and accuracy limitations.

decision_reason: The breadth is potentially useful, but explicit permission is required and the dated, self-qualified manual needs a comprehensive claim-level safety and duplication audit before corpus use.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not copy, store, transform, translate, or queue the manual body without written permission from Pete Riggs for derivative corpus, retrieval-system, and training use. Retain only citation metadata, author, title, version/date, page count, canonical URL, exact rights restriction, and permission requirement. If permission is granted, perform a complete specialist safety, provenance, visual-dependence, and duplication review before any extraction; preserve author caveats, source-label opinion, and exclude unsafe, unsupported, outdated, or image-only material.

source_name: Rope Bondage and Affective Embodiments
discovered_from: scholarly rope embodiment and culture search
access_notes: The journal landing and full HTML are public and robots-allowed, and the article has DOI 10.14483/25909398.14228. The item displays CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 and copyright, so commercial use and derivative transformations are not licensed; an information-dense training Markdown synthesis requires author/editor permission.

representative_pages.note: Official 2019 article record and public HTML for multi-site fieldwork in Hong Kong, London, Paris, and Berlin on affect, identity, flow, materiality, and cultural narratives.
Persistent DOI identity for the journal article.

decision_reason: The ethnography adds unusually broad comparative evidence and critiques simplistic origin narratives, but the NoDerivatives and noncommercial license does not authorize the intended transformed training corpus.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not download, transform, translate, queue, or train on the article body under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 without written author/editor permission covering derivatives and the intended use. Retain only bibliographic metadata, DOI, public citation, license, and permission requirement. If cleared, preserve field sites, methods, participant/evidence type, affect, identity, flow, materiality, cultural framing, critique of rigid feudal-origin narratives, limitations, and bibliography while avoiding long participant quotations and universalized interpretation; do not use ResearchGate or third-party copies.

source_name: On Kinbaku and Shibari: Japanese Rope Art under Globalization

representative_pages.note: Official metadata provides author, title, conference, year, DOI, and Abstract License Flag: Disallowed.
Persistent DOI resolves to J-STAGE article metadata whose generic *_pdf route is not an authorized crawler endpoint.

decision_reason: The citation is a useful scholarly lead on globalization, but its one-page body is neither licensed nor available through a permitted capture route.
recommended_crawl_scope: Retain metadata only: title, author, conference, year, DOI, canonical CiNii URL, Abstract License Flag: Disallowed, and permission/access requirement. Do not capture or translate the abstract, access J-STAGE *_pdf, use a mirror, or queue the body. Reconsider only if the author or publisher supplies a robots-permitted copy with written permission for derivative corpus and training use.

source_name: Remedial Ropes WordPress Archive
access_notes: The robots-allowed landing page is public but says the site is under construction. All six promised 2012 article bodies currently return 404; the sitemap/feed exposes only the landing shell and empty/dead post references. No archive, cache, mirror, or search snippet was used as source content.

representative_pages.note: Live under-construction landing page lists six dead article routes but contains no substantive rope facts.
Promised overview article currently returns 404.
Promised nerve article currently returns 404, as do the flyer, flyer text, myths, and references bodies.

decision_reason: The live site contains only a topic inventory and dead URLs, while its substantive successor is already represented by the evidence-gated Twisted Windows candidate.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not queue the landing page, dead URLs, URL titles, feed, sitemap, search snippets, cache, archive, or mirror. Retain only the canonical URL, current under-construction and 404 status, six-body audit, successor relationship to Twisted Windows, and rejection rationale. Recheck only if the rightsholder restores substantive first-party bodies under a reviewable access and rights policy.

source_name: House Cordee Rope Bottoming Advice
access_notes: Nineteen substantive articles are publicly readable through robots-allowed Squarespace routes. Each article explicitly says the content is copyrighted and asks readers not to copy it elsewhere, permitting link sharing instead. Pages display 'Written By Peter Martin' while saying the series is written by Mya and Fox; that attribution ambiguity must be resolved before reuse.

representative_pages.note: Detailed bottom-led framework connects personal risk factors, consequence severity, privacy, work, support, and evolving limits without claiming risk can be eliminated.
Practitioner guidance covers urgent escalation, notification, accountability, injury documentation, scene planning, and recovery, mixed with uncited medical assertions.
Distinguishes warm-up from flexibility training but makes exercise and injury-risk claims that require professional corroboration.

recommended_crawl_scope: Do not queue, copy, summarize, derive QA from, or obtain the body through commercial sites, caches, archives, or third-party mirrors. Retain only the official citation, public-release statement, unreachable route, 2012-conversion status, explicit unchanged-since-1995 and potentially-outdated-source warnings, and rejection rationale. Do not use its fiber or wire rope, knots, splices, lashings, slings, blocks, mechanical advantage, anchorages, guylines, or improvised lifting structures for bondage, performer flying, human suspension, tree or indoor hardpoints, equipment selection, load limits, or rescue; prefer current manufacturer, engineering, rescue, and the accepted 2024 USFS sources.

source_name: New River Gorge Climbing Guide Certification Guidelines

access_notes: The official National Park Service page is publicly accessible through a robots-allowed route and was last updated 8 December 2017. Federal page text is generally public domain; the representative photograph and any third-party names or marks are excluded.

representative_pages.note: Official competency map names bowline, figure-eight variants, overhand, water knot, double fisherman's, munter and prussic; belay-device categories; anchor position, assessment, redundancy and load distribution; natural/artificial anchors; backup belays; rappelling; ascending/assistance; pulley raising; equipment inspection; environmental hazards; and measured proficiency.
Current NPS policy disallows search and loader routes but not the public park curriculum page.

decision_reason: The concise federal page is useful as a professional competency and assessment map for knots, anchors, redundancy, inspection, assistance, and environmental awareness, not as a technique tutorial.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create a compact source-attributed curriculum map preserving its specific New River Gorge permit-equivalency context, 24-hour minimum plus proficiency exam, named knot families, backup instruction, belay-device categories, anchor position and assessment, redundancy and load distribution, natural-versus-artificial anchors, connector loading awareness, backup belays, rappelling, ascending to assist, pulley raising, equipment inspection/replacement, and environmental hazards. Exclude the photograph, marks, examples that function as device endorsement, and all construction or operational steps. Do not claim the page is current AMGA curriculum, certify any person, knot, anchor, hardpoint, tree, rescue system, bondage setup, or human suspension, or directly transfer climbing standards; use it only to inform pedagogy and the need for instruction plus demonstrated assessment.

source_name: Heating Characteristics of Cordage Fibers, NBS Report 8308
access_notes: NIST provides official metadata, DOI 10.6028/NBS.RPT.8308, and an exact legacy PDF for the 10 March 1964 National Bureau of Standards report, approved for public release on 9 October 2015. NIST states that unmarked agency information is public and may be copied or distributed; any marked copyrighted or third-party material remains excluded.

representative_pages.note: Official report metadata, author, date, DOI, release status, and abstract.
Official report studies heat of wetting and self-heating for three abaca and one Haitian sisal fiber samples in bulk-storage or bale contexts.
NIST policy permits copying and distribution of unmarked agency information while preserving restrictions on marked third-party material.

decision_reason: The released federal report supplies rare primary measurements and modeling context for wetting-related heating in bulk cordage fibers, with strong limits on rope-care transfer.
recommended_crawl_scope: Capture a method-preserving digest of sample identity, heat-of-wetting experiments, self-heating observations, bulk-bale or storage-pile context, modeled assumptions, assumed material properties, extrapolation limits, and the report's historical date. Keep abaca and Haitian sisal distinct and do not generalize results to jute, finished rope, individual coils, or bondage use. Exclude singeing temperatures, boiling or wet-processing guidance, oil or wax treatment, storage guarantees, fire thresholds presented as modern rules, individual-rope strength or care, and any claim that one preparation method is safe. Audit any third-party table or figure before use and prefer text-only federal content.
rights_basis: Unmarked NIST/NBS federal information in the officially released report may be copied and distributed; marked third-party material is excluded.

source_name: NIOSH Alert: Preventing Worker Injuries and Deaths from Suspension Scaffold Failures
access_notes: CDC/NIOSH provides the public 1992 Alert and describes five incidents causing six deaths, including burned rope, inadequate counterweight, unsecured outriggers, loose U-bolts, and a failure after two weeks of normal use. CDC says most agency material is public domain but its reuse guidance also says not to make substantive changes; that condition conflicts with the corpus contract's dense original paraphrase requirement. The cited 1992 regulatory context is also obsolete.

representative_pages.note: Official incident Alert with five case summaries, hazard controls, inspection, independent anchorage, and heat/corrosion/sharp-edge protection.
CDC reuse guidance states most agency information is public domain but requires attribution, no implied endorsement, continued free availability, and no substantive changes.

decision_reason: The federal incident evidence is valuable, but the no-substantive-changes reuse condition and obsolete regulatory context need a documented policy decision before transformed training use.
recommended_crawl_scope: Retain citation, date, agency, incident count, public-domain statement, no-substantive-changes condition, and a rights-policy review task only. Do not queue or transform the body until CDC confirms that dense original paraphrase for the intended corpus is permitted. If cleared, preserve incident-derived evidence, separate incident facts from recommendations, update or omit 1992 regulations and body-belt terminology, and capture only general inspection, separately secured support, and heat/corrosion/sharp-edge protection concepts. Do not reproduce identifiable incident narrative for drama, reconstruct scaffold operation, or transfer construction, counterweight, outrigger, anchorage, load, or rope guidance into bondage or human suspension.
rights_basis: Most CDC material is public domain, but current CDC reuse guidance prohibits substantive changes; transformed training use is deferred pending clarification.

Do not derive a safe rope placement, pressure, duration, release time, recovery window, function test, or treatment; do not claim that any symptom confirms rope-related neuropathy; advise qualified medical evaluation for concerning neurologic symptoms.

representative_pages.note: Official versioned record for a broad knot-chart PDF; page is all-rights-reserved and graphics must not be copied.
Official publication hub intermittently returned a verification page; no bypass was attempted.
Terminology lead for knots and splices; route returned a verification page during final audit.
Journal and archive lead requiring separate access and permission review.

decision_reason: The guild is a high-value authority and bibliography lead, but its charts and journal are ordinary copyright and key routes are challenge-gated; written derivative and training permission is required.
recommended_crawl_scope: Retain official citation, chart filename/version/date, public issue and publication metadata, page titles, organization identity, copyright notice, challenge status, and a permission request. Do not download, copy, transform, translate, queue, transcribe, infer steps from diagrams, enter member areas, or bypass verification. If permission and ordinary access are obtained, manually allowlist text-supported knot taxonomy, terminology, history, and source-attributed technical discussion; preserve issue, author, date, material, geometry, test conditions, disagreement, and uncertainty. Exclude images and diagrams without separate rights, anonymous forum consensus, member-only material, decorative-project instructions, and direct transfer into body restraint, hardpoints, human suspension, or safety certification.
rights_basis: Official page states all rights reserved and no open license was found; chart, journal, images, and third-party contributions require written permission and item-level rights review.

Exclude courses, Patreon-only bodies, Discord, merch, promotion, AI-consent material unless independently scoped, individualized legal or clinical claims, and any implication that one framework proves consent or resolves an incident.

source_name: NBS Technologic Paper T300: Standard Bending Test for Rope Yarns
access_notes: The official 12-page NIST archive copy is a 1925 Bureau of Standards primary study by Charles W. Schoffstall and Robert C. Boyden. It explains why breaking-strength tests miss repeated bending, develops a controlled rope-yarn flex-fatigue apparatus, studies twist preservation, humidity, load, bend geometry, speed, variability, and replication, and is a NIST Technical Series work eligible for broad derivative reuse subject to authorship and third-party review.

representative_pages.note: Official complete 1925 report, method, results, limitations, and publication context.
Official NIST series index binding T300, authors, title, and DOI 10.6028/nbst.8322.
NIST Technical Series policy grants worldwide reprint and derivative rights for employee-authored works while warning that third-party works may remain protected.

decision_reason: The primary federal report adds a missing evidence concept—repeated bending and test-design variability are not represented by simple breaking strength—while its age and manila rope-yarn scope can be tightly preserved.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create a source-attributed historical-method digest from the official NIST PDF and series metadata. Preserve the 1925 date; authors; DOI 10.6028/nbst.8322; Bureau of Standards context; distinction between finished rope and rope yarn; reason breaking-strength tests do not represent repeated bending; study of tension, bend geometry, speed, humidity, twist preservation, variability, replication, and distribution rather than average alone; sample identity as No. 18 manila yarn from a specified rope; and all stated limits. Exclude apparatus blueprints, exact operational construction instructions, obsolete procurement specifications, figures unless separately audited, credited third-party apparatus content, and numerical recipes presented as current standards. Do not generalize manila rope-yarn results to finished rope, jute, hemp, synthetic uplines, wet or treated rope, bondage, knots, safety factors, retirement, care, or human suspension, and do not infer current certification or performance thresholds.
rights_basis: NIST Technical Series employee-authored works are not protected domestically under 17 U.S.C. § 105 and NIST grants broad worldwide reprint and derivative rights; third-party or credited components remain excluded pending item-level audit.

source_name: Tensile Strength of Ropes and Friction Hitch Used in Tree Climbing Work
discovered_from: friction-hitch mechanics and tree-climbing rope review
access_notes: The official open-access article, DOI 10.3390/f12111457, was published October 26, 2021 under CC BY 4.0. It reports 36 slow-pull tests across two specified synthetic semi-static arborist rope systems and two friction-hitch constructions, Prusik and Valdotain tresse, with nine replicates per treatment and measurements of load, slip, diameter, temperature, and statistical variation. The authors frame the work as preliminary and explicitly say the tested system is not insurance against a dynamic event.

representative_pages.note: Official article body, methods, results, limitations, publication date, DOI, and CC BY 4.0 notice.
Persistent publisher DOI identity.
License terms for attributed adaptation and redistribution.

decision_reason: This is a rights-clear primary mechanics study with unusually explicit rope-system identity, replication, multiple measurements, statistics, and limits; it adds evidence about friction-hitch behavior without pretending to identify a universally best knot.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create a manually paraphrased, claim-cited evidence digest under CC BY 4.0. Preserve the exact two arborist synthetic semi-static rope systems; Prusik and Valdotain tresse distinction; 36 total tests and nine replicates per treatment; slow-pull, short-stroke bench method; modified-standard status; measured load, slip, diameter, temperature, and statistical variation; stated purpose; preliminary status; and call for dynamic and cyclic work. Exclude images, trademarks, third-party components, unsafe tying recipes, and decontextualized thresholds or ratings. Do not generalize the tested arborist equipment, short quasi-static bench stroke, or results to natural fiber, bondage rope, other diameters or constructions, dynamic or cyclic loading, falls, body contact, safe working loads, human suspension, or a recommendation that either hitch is best or safe.
rights_basis: The official MDPI article is licensed CC BY 4.0; preserve attribution, DOI, license link, change indication, and a component-level audit for images or third-party material.

source_name: Fatigue and Weatherability Studies of Aramid Fiber Rope Slings
discovered_from: rope fatigue, weathering, elongation, and end-fitting review
access_notes: The official NIST record and 44-page NBSIR 76-1159 PDF identify Nixon Halsey and Leonard Mordfin's September 1976 study, DOI 10.6028/NBS.IR.76-1159. It tested 26 prototype aramid sling-leg specimens spanning parallel-strand and cabled-strand constructions and three nominal capacity families under a fatigue spectrum and simulated weathering, with residual strength, permanent elongation, and end-fitting observations. Inadequate manufacturer-supplied end fittings prevented a thorough evaluation, and the report says its concluding observations are not fully substantiated.

representative_pages.note: Official title, authors, date, report number, DOI, and abstract-level scope.
Official complete report with methods, results, equipment limitations, and conclusion caveats.
Current NIST federal-information reuse statement and third-party warning.

decision_reason: The primary federal study adds rare material on fatigue-spectrum design, simulated weathering, residual strength, permanent elongation, construction, and end-fitting failure as an experimental confound, provided its prototype helicopter-sling and inconclusive scope remains explicit.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create a source-attributed historical evidence digest from the official NIST record and report. Preserve NBSIR 76-1159, DOI 10.6028/NBS.IR.76-1159, authors, September 1976 date, 26 prototype aramid sling-leg specimens, parallel-strand versus cabled-strand constructions, three capacity families, fatigue-spectrum and simulated-weathering design, residual-strength and permanent-elongation measurements, end-fitting observations, and the report's statement that inadequate manufacturer end fittings prevented thorough evaluation and left conclusions incompletely substantiated. Exclude numeric load or rating recipes, end-fitting design suggestions, helicopter operations, figures and tables pending component audit, vendor claims, and obsolete procurement detail. Do not transfer prototype aramid helicopter external-cargo sling results to natural-fiber bondage rope, other synthetic rope, knots, uplines, care or retirement rules, body contact, safe working loads, or human suspension.
rights_basis: Official unmarked NIST/NBS federal employee text is generally public information under 17 U.S.C. § 105 and NIST's current reuse statement; marked, credited, vendor, or third-party components remain excluded.

source_name: UK Maritime and Coastguard Agency Work at Height and Lifting Guidance
discovered_from: current government rope-access, load-path, rescue, and lifting-system review
access_notes: Two current MCA Marine Guidance Notes provide complementary system-level evidence under the Open Government Licence v3.0. MGN 410 amendment 3 was published July 24, 2024 and covers work-at-height risk assessment, safer-method hierarchy, competence, supervision, rescue planning, equipment suitability, and limited conditions for rope access. MGN 332 amendment 4 was published January 7, 2026 and covers system design, selection, maintenance, competent examination, records, lifting persons, and mounts, fixings, and attachments as parts of the load path. Both are maritime regulatory guidance, not bondage standards.

representative_pages.note: July 2024 MGN 410 amendment 3, current work-at-height planning and rope-access boundaries.
January 2026 MGN 332 amendment 4, current whole-system lifting, examination, record, and load-path framing.
OGL v3.0 terms for eligible Crown text.

decision_reason: The current OGL source family adds concise whole-system, load-path, competence, examination, planning, and rescue concepts, but only at a general transferable level with maritime law and operations removed.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create one source-family digest of eligible Crown text from MGN 410 amendment 3 (July 24, 2024) and MGN 332 amendment 4 (January 7, 2026). Preserve source versions; maritime context; risk assessment and safer-method hierarchy; competence, training, supervision, and rescue planning; equipment suitability and foreseeable-load framing; rope access as a conditional method; ergonomic support concept; whole-system design and selection; maintenance, competent examination, and records; lifting-person caution; and mounts, fixings, attachments, and supporting structure as parts of the load path. Exclude logos, images, third-party material, regulation or legal text, jurisdiction-specific duties, numeric intervals, loads, ratings, operational procedures, certification claims, and equipment prescriptions. Do not transfer maritime requirements to bondage, certify a rope-access or lifting method, or treat the guidance as definitive structural or human-suspension approval; it expressly requires circumstance-specific competent judgment.
rights_basis: Eligible Crown text on GOV.UK is available under the Open Government Licence v3.0; exclude logos, personal data, linked standards, and any third-party material not covered by that licence.

source_name: UK Maritime and Coastguard Agency Tensioned-Rope Hazard Guidance
discovered_from: tensioned rope, bight, redirect, and system hazard review
access_notes: MGN 592 amendment 2 was published October 14, 2024 under OGL v3.0. It treats anchoring, mooring, towing, and hauling equipment as complete systems; identifies the entire load path through machinery, bollards, fairleads, attachments, and support structure; and discusses tensioned-rope bights, snap-back, snagged-line release, redirects, hazard zones, maintenance, repair competence, communication, oversight, and load reduction. Its maritime loads and operations are radically different from rope bondage or human suspension.

representative_pages.note: Official October 2024 current guidance body, system boundaries, tensioned-line hazards, and OGL notice.
Official publication-family landing and amendment identity.
OGL v3.0 terms for eligible Crown text.

decision_reason: A very narrow OGL digest can add general system, bight, tension, redirect, snag-release, and communication hazard concepts that are often missing from rope instruction, while excluding all maritime operations and numerical transfer.
recommended_crawl_scope: Capture only eligible Crown text expressing general hazard-system concepts: assess the system as a whole; trace the complete load path; include machinery, contact and redirect points, attachments, and support structure; inspect and maintain components; keep expert repair boundaries; recognize tensioned-rope bights, snap-back, snagged-line sudden release, and redirect or fairlead changes to a hazard zone; reduce load when strain is excessive; and maintain communication, oversight, and exclusion practices. Preserve the October 14, 2024 amendment identity and maritime mooring, towing, anchoring, and hauling context. Exclude logos, images, third-party material, law, all numerical loads, speeds, dimensions, rope sizing, turns, line layouts, stoppering, heaving-line construction, operational instructions, equipment selection, ratings, and legal duties. Do not transfer massive-vessel-load behavior or maritime procedures into bondage, anchors, hardpoints, upline operation, body contact, rescue, or human suspension.
rights_basis: Eligible Crown text on GOV.UK is available under the Open Government Licence v3.0; logos, linked standards, personal data, and third-party components are excluded.

source_name: Basic Statistical Properties of the Knot Efficiency
access_notes: The official Symmetry article, DOI 10.3390/sym14091926, was published September 15, 2022 under CC BY 4.0. It treats knot efficiency as a ratio of two heterogeneous breaking-strength random variables rather than one sharp number, derives probability and cumulative distributions, and discusses mean, variance, median, mode, tolerance intervals, and approximations. Its empirical illustration reuses earlier measurements, approximately 200 straight-rope and 80 knotted-rope breaks; the paper reports no new public dataset.

representative_pages.note: Official full article, mathematical method, evidence provenance, DOI, date, and CC BY 4.0 notice.
Persistent publisher DOI identity.

decision_reason: The article adds a durable corrective to false single-value knot-efficiency claims and teaches why replication, variance, compatible methods, and uncertainty matter, while overlapping the 2020 experiment enough to require a narrow methods-only extraction.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create a claim-cited statistical-method digest under CC BY 4.0. Preserve the ratio definition; distinction between measured breaking strengths and calculated efficiency; rope heterogeneity; why a single paired break and a ratio of small-sample means are misleading; the random-variable framing; PDF, CDF, central-tendency, dispersion, tolerance-interval, and approximation concepts in plain language; the approximately 200 straight-rope and 80 knotted-rope provenance from earlier work; and the difference between failure to reject normality and proof of normality. Exclude equations as operational calculators, tables and figures pending component audit, broad unsupported statements that every rope breaks at a knot, the claim that almost all prior work is wrong, numeric efficiency ranges or worked values as ratings, and any knot selection, safe-load, or safety-factor recipe. Do not generalize the model beyond its assumptions or to dynamic, cyclic, wet, aged, damaged, natural-fiber, bondage, body-contact, or human-suspension conditions.
rights_basis: The official MDPI article is CC BY 4.0; preserve attribution, DOI, license link, change indication, and audit figures, tables, references, and third-party components separately.

source_name: Tensile Properties of Pleated Synthetic Rope

access_notes: The official NIST record and 48-page scan identify NBSIR 86-3375, S. George Fattal, April 1986 and issue date September 1986, DOI 10.6028/NBS.IR.86-3375. Twenty approximately five-metre pleated nylon ropes in two very large nominal sizes were monotonically tensioned to rupture; untreated, heated, and cooled groups supplied breaking-strength, elongation, load-deformation, and failure-mode observations for a US Army mired-vehicle kinetic-recovery program. The report explicitly calls for unresolved cyclic, connection, moisture, debris, and field testing.

source_name: Tangled Physics: Knots Strain Intuitive Physical Reasoning
discovered_from: knot verification and pedagogy evidence review
access_notes: The 2024 Open Mind article is bound by PMID 39439589, PMCID PMC11495958, and DOI 10.1162/opmi_a_00159. Licensed JATS from the robots-allowed EMBL-EBI Europe PMC API states CC BY 4.0. Across five experiments, observers judged a deliberately controlled four-knot series using photographs, renderings, videos, diagrams, and a topology-matching control; judgments did not track the documented strength hierarchy even when participants could distinguish topology. The task concerns simple bends and resistance to coming undone under pulling, not comprehensive knot safety or bondage expertise.

The authors call one knot the safest while also saying future work must address dynamics, other symmetry, functionality, and untying; they withhold underlying data because misinterpretation could cause fall accidents.

Persistent publisher DOI identity.

source_name: A Statistical Analysis of Some Mechanical Properties of Manila Rope

access_notes: The official NIST legacy publication is NBS Research Paper RP1847 by Sanford B. Newman and I. H. It analyzes 863 observations from more than 800 government-purchase samples of three-strand Manila rope acquired from 1938 through 1941; four-strand data were insufficient. The paper explicitly treats the sample universe as heterogeneous and nonrandom and discusses variability from fiber quality, fabrication, and previous treatment.
