decision_reason: The study may add rare autistic lived-experience evidence, but it is a six-person interpretive sample, the repository grants only noncommercial use, and direct body review is currently challenge-blocked. Participant narratives and identity generalizations require especially careful handling.
recommended_crawl_scope: Retain title, authors, year, DOI, journal, peer-review status, six-adult purposive social-media sample, one-to-one spoken interviews, interpretative phenomenological analysis, communication or consent or sensory or normativity topics, accepted-manuscript status, noncommercial self-archiving restriction, access challenge, and permission lead as metadata only. Do not bypass the challenge, copy, paraphrase, transform, translate, queue, or reproduce participant narratives or quotations. Seek commercial derivative and model-training permission and legitimate body access. If cleared, create a minimal claim-cited methods-and-findings digest that preserves sample size, self-selection, interviewer and interpretive positionality, and non-generalizability; never claim all autistic people prefer BDSM, that BDSM spaces are inherently safe, or that lived experience supplies medical, sensory, consent, tying or safety rules.
rights_basis: The Durham accepted-manuscript notice authorizes noncommercial use under publisher self-archiving terms; commercial transformation and intended model-training compatibility are not assumed, and current body access is challenge-blocked.

source_name: Characterization of Plant-Based Natural Fiber Ropes for Potential Futuristic Application as Reinforcement in Concrete Structures
access_notes: The 2024 Journal of Natural Fibers article, DOI 10.1080/15440478.2024.2397703, is CC BY 4.0 and compares jute, nettle, coir, sisal and cotton ropes before and after alkali and hydrothermal or boiling-water treatments for concrete reinforcement. Its own limitation notes differences in source, age, growth area, extraction, processing and manufacture. The full web body exposes high-consequence chemical and boiling recipes, numerical rankings and treatment claims that could easily contaminate bondage-rope care advice.

representative_pages.note: Publisher body with title, authors, DOI, CC BY notice, five plant-rope types, treatment methods, physical or chemical or mechanical or thermal characterization, findings, limitations, disclosure and request-only data.
DOAJ identity and authorship cross-check for the open article.
Persistent DOI identity for the article.
Article-stated Attribution license.

decision_reason: The directly relevant natural-rope scope is outweighed by severe care-contamination risk: chemical and boiling treatments intended for concrete reinforcement cannot become bondage-rope cleaning or performance advice. Independent textile and safety adjudication is required before any body use.
recommended_crawl_scope: Retain title, authors, year, DOI, journal, CC BY 4.0 license, jute or nettle or coir or sisal or cotton rope inventory, untreated-versus-alkali-versus-hydrothermal experimental comparison, concrete-reinforcement purpose, physical or morphological or chemical or mechanical or thermal characterization categories, differences in source or age or growth area or extraction or processing or manufacture, request-only data, funding and no-conflict statement as metadata only. Do not copy, paraphrase, transform, queue, or derive chemical concentrations, boiling or hydrothermal recipes, timing, temperatures, washing, drying, treatment outcomes, diameter or lay changes, strength or thermal rankings, values, figures, tables, supplier or material recommendations, or any care, cleaning, conditioning, sterilization, repair, retirement, body-contact, knot, upline, anchor or human-suspension advice. Require independent natural-textile engineering and bondage-rope safety review before reconsideration.
rights_basis: The publisher body states CC BY 4.0, but open licensing does not resolve severe domain-transfer and treatment-recipe risk; no body is queued before specialist safety adjudication.

source_name: Deadheavy Rope School Resources
access_notes: The page is a short directory of roughly 21 outbound resources, books, podcasts, event or community references, quotations and recommendations rather than a factual source body. It also says a generic neurodynamic exercise can promote nerve health without supplying rope-specific evidence. The robots file sets a ten-second crawl delay, but no reuse license or terms grant is visible. A linked code of conduct has useful boundaries but says it was adapted from other organizations, leaving a layered rights chain.

representative_pages.note: Actual directory page with outbound links, books, quotations and brief recommendations; minimal first-party factual content.
Useful educator or participant boundaries but an expressly adapted text with unclear upstream rights.
First-party crawl-delay signal; crawl permission is not a reuse license.

decision_reason: This is a discovery directory, not a corpus body. Memorizing its URLs would recreate the exact URL-trivia failure the dataset is meant to avoid, while copying its adapted code of conduct would ignore layered rights.
recommended_crawl_scope: Do not queue, mirror, copy, paraphrase, transform, or create URL-recall training from this page. Retain outbound URLs only as unaudited discovery leads and evaluate each destination on its own evidence, rights, access and safety merits. Do not import quotations, book descriptions, event or course advertising, generic neurodynamic-exercise claims, or the adapted code of conduct without written permission from every relevant rights holder.
rights_basis: The public page shows no reuse license; robots access does not grant republication or model-training rights, and the potentially useful code-of-conduct text has an explicitly layered adaptation history.

source_name: Access, Choice, and Pleasure: Towards a New Model of Disability Sexual Rights
discovered_from: disability agency and accessibility evidence gap
access_notes: The complete 2024 Sexuality and Disability article by Rina B. Pikkel is publicly readable as HTML and PDF under CC BY 4.0. It is a theoretical and legal framework centered on access, choice, pleasure, heterogeneity and agency, not an empirical study or rope-specific accessibility protocol; its legal discussion is substantially grounded in Israeli and international contexts.

representative_pages.note: Complete article, author and affiliation, publication history, theoretical framework, funding acknowledgement and CC BY 4.0 license.
Persistent DOI identity for the article.
Article-stated Attribution license.

decision_reason: The access-choice-pleasure framework adds a rights-based, agency-preserving model for accessibility and avoids reducing disabled people to risk or incapacity. Its value is conceptual and must retain its theoretical and jurisdictional limits.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create an attributed CC BY 4.0 theory-and-limitations digest covering the three-part access, choice and pleasure framework; heterogeneity among disabled people; agency; barriers; and the distinction between negative protection and positive access conditions. Preserve author, year, DOI, institutional affiliation, theoretical rather than empirical status, Israeli and international legal context, funding, license and changes. Exclude legal advice, jurisdiction-specific conclusions presented as universal law, lengthy historical narrative, third-party quotations or images, any claim that the paper validates a rope adaptation, and any implication that a person has a right to another person's sexual participation. Translate the framework only into nonprescriptive accessibility, scene-design and anti-deficit curriculum questions.
rights_basis: The article explicitly states CC BY 4.0; preserve full attribution, DOI, license and change indication, and audit or exclude separately credited components.

source_name: A thin line between consent and abuse—Reflections on sexual experiences among Swedish young adults with intellectual disabilities
access_notes: The CC BY 4.0 article is available through Europe PMC and an EMBL-EBI full-text XML route permitted for article access. It reports 22 semi-structured interviews with Swedish adults aged 18 to 35, including 17 women and 5 men, recruited through day-activity centers and specialized upper-secondary schools; inclusion required sufficient verbal communication. Thematic analysis used sexual-script theory, and the sensitive, selected, gender-imbalanced sample cannot represent all people with intellectual disabilities.

representative_pages.note: Article identity, authors, publication metadata, abstract and CC BY status.
Robots-permitted EMBL-EBI full-text XML containing methods, findings, limitations, funding and license.
Article-stated Attribution license.

decision_reason: This methods-bounded qualitative study adds rare direct evidence about agency, contextual ambiguity, clarification and accessible consent communication. Participant privacy, selection limits and Swedish context require a narrow digest rather than narrative reuse or generalization.
recommended_crawl_scope: Use only the authorized EMBL-EBI article body to create a claim-cited methods, findings and limitations digest. Preserve the 22-interview design, 17-women and 5-men composition, age range, Swedish recruitment routes, sufficient-verbal-communication inclusion criterion, thematic analysis, sexual-script framing, agency and self-awareness theme, contextual ambiguity, clarification, accessible communication or education need, and consent-versus-abuse boundary. Exclude every participant quotation or story, identifying detail, diagnosis or legal-capacity advice, prevalence inference, deficit framing, generalization to all people with intellectual disabilities, and BDSM- or rope-specific claims. Use the evidence only to support accessible communication, context sensitivity and epistemic humility.
rights_basis: The article and EMBL-EBI full-text XML state CC BY 4.0; preserve attribution, license, change indication and study limitations, and exclude participant quotations and separately credited components.

source_name: BSEE Safety Alert 454: Auxiliary Line Abrading Causes Rope Guard Failure
discovered_from: rope contact-path and inspection evidence gap
access_notes: The official one-page December 14, 2022 BSEE alert and PDF are publicly accessible. It describes chronic scraping of an auxiliary wire rope against adjacent components, damage to a side plate, pin boss, rope guard and line, a dropped guard near workers, and a high-speed line moving to an adjacent sheave. It is an industrial crane near-miss involving wire rope, vendor equipment and separately owned photos, not textile or body rope.

representative_pages.note: Official alert landing page and federal incident summary.
One-page official PDF with incident chain, recommendations and photos that require component-level rights review.
DOI copyright policy explains the public-domain status of federal material and separate rights in some components.

decision_reason: The compact official incident supplies a useful contact-path and adjacent-component failure chain plus line-of-fire lessons. It must remain explicitly industrial and qualitative because wire-rope and crane behavior does not establish textile-rope or human-suspension behavior.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create a short, text-only public-domain incident-chain digest. Preserve the sequence from chronic auxiliary-line scraping or rubbing to damage of the side plate, pin boss, rope guard and line, the guard's fall near workers, and movement of the high-speed line to an adjacent sheave; retain the need to inspect contact paths and adjacent system components and to consider dropped-object and line-of-fire consequences. Exclude both photos, vendor or crane model, exact height, API text, replacement-carrier design prescription, inspection intervals, offshore crane procedure, and every direct inference to textile rope, natural fiber, knots, bondage, body contact, anchors, uplines or human suspension.
rights_basis: Eligible BSEE-authored federal text is public domain in the United States; preserve attribution and exclude photographs, trademarks, standards text and any separately owned component.

source_name: Kinky Sex and Deliberate Partner Negotiations: Case Studies of Canadian Transgender Men Who Have Sex with Men
access_notes: The 2022 IJERPH article is available under CC BY 4.0 through a robots-permitted EMBL-EBI Europe PMC full-text XML route. It presents approximately one-hour semi-structured interviews with three HIV-negative transgender men who have sex with men, all aged at least 40 and based in Toronto, as part of community-based participatory research with ethics approval. Its negotiation and safety themes are participant perceptions from three selected cases, not causal evidence or a general safer-sex protocol.

representative_pages.note: Canonical publisher identity, DOI and article-level CC BY license.
Robots-permitted EMBL-EBI article body with methods, community involvement, ethics, findings and limitations.
Persistent DOI identity for the article.

decision_reason: The three cases add direct but very narrow accounts of deliberate partner negotiation, explicit consent, sobriety, affirming partners and community support. These are useful as attributed perceptions only and cannot prove that kink is inherently safe or protective.
recommended_crawl_scope: Use only the authorized EMBL-EBI article body to create a compact methods-and-perceptions digest. Preserve the three-person Toronto case-study design, age floor, approximately one-hour semi-structured interviews, community-based participatory research and advisory-board context, ethics and electronic-consent protections, pseudonymization, CIHR funding, no-conflict statement, and participant-reported themes of deliberate negotiation, clearer communication or explicit consent, trust, sobriety, gender-affirming partners and community support. Exclude every participant quotation, story and unnecessary demographic detail; HIV clinical advice; causal or protective-effect claims; generalization to transgender men, MSM or kink communities; and any claim that kink, SSC or RACK is proven safer. Label every substantive result as a perception from one or more of three selected cases.
rights_basis: The article and authorized EMBL-EBI body state CC BY 4.0; preserve attribution, DOI, license, changes, methods and limitations, and exclude participant quotations and separately credited components.

source_name: MAIB Report 13/2017: Zarga mooring-line failure
discovered_from: textile-rope inspection uncertainty and load-path evidence gap
access_notes: The GOV.UK landing page and 116-page MAIB Report 13/2017 are publicly accessible under Crown copyright and the Open Government Licence framework. The investigation concerns a deck officer severely injured when an HMPE ship-mooring line parted; it analyzes axial-compression fatigue, cyclic and contact damage, a jacket obscuring the load-bearing core, visual-inspection limits, incompatible deck fittings, incomplete supplier or builder information, and transfer of an offshore test method. Credited photographs, annexes, supplier tests and other third-party components require exclusion or separate review.

representative_pages.note: Official report landing page, occurrence and publication dates, summary and Crown-copyright terms.
Complete 116-page official report with government-authored investigation, figures, annexes and credited third-party components.
Open Government Licence version 3 terms for eligible Crown material.

decision_reason: This is the strongest new evidence on textile-rope inspection uncertainty: construction, hidden-core damage, contact geometry, component compatibility, information flow and invalid method transfer combine in a documented severe-injury failure. Its maritime scale and third-party components require a strict qualitative allowlist.
recommended_crawl_scope: Manually isolate roughly 20 to 30 rope-relevant pages of government-authored findings and create a claim-cited OGL digest. Preserve the documented severe-injury context; HMPE construction; axial-compression, cyclic, bending and contact damage; the jacket obscuring the load-bearing core and discard criteria; visual inspections that judged the line good despite low residual strength; line-and-deck-fitting incompatibility; incomplete supplier, builder and operator information; whole-system load-path and communication lessons; and the error of transferring an offshore-sector test methodology to ship mooring. Exclude names and personal details, all photos, credited annexes and vendor test reports, product identity, standards or legal guidance, exact loads, MBL, ratios, cycles, life estimates and retirement numbers, snapback-zone recipes, and any equivalence to natural fiber, bondage rope, body contact, anchors, uplines or human suspension.
rights_basis: Eligible Crown-authored report text is reusable under the Open Government Licence version 3; preserve source attribution and changes, and exclude credited photographs, annexes, supplier reports, trademarks and any separately owned component.

source_name: NPS Museum Handbook Appendix K: Curatorial Care of Textile Objects
discovered_from: natural-fiber construction and aging evidence gap
access_notes: The official 50-page National Park Service Museum Handbook appendix is publicly accessible and federal-authored. Pages K:2 through K:13 distinguish animal, plant and synthetic fibers, describe plant cellulose and bast or leaf or seed categories, explain processing effects and molecular-chain breakdown, and summarize hygroscopic dimensional stress, light, heat, moisture, pollution, pest and abrasion deterioration. It is a 2002 museum-textile guide, not a rope-strength, hygiene, care or retirement standard.

representative_pages.note: Official 50-page Appendix K containing fiber construction, processing, deterioration, museum-preservation instructions, figures and references.
NPS disclaimer and component-level copyright notice for federal and separately owned material.

decision_reason: This public federal handbook adds a clear fiber-to-deterioration mechanism vocabulary, especially for plant cellulose and processing damage. Museum context and age require strict separation from operational bondage-rope care, strength and retirement advice.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create a text-only public-domain mechanism digest from approximately pages K:2 through K:13. Preserve animal-versus-plant-versus-synthetic fiber distinctions; plant cellulose and bast, leaf or seed groupings; processing effects; agents of deterioration; molecular-chain breakdown and brittleness; hygroscopic dimensional change and mechanical stress; and qualitative effects of light or ultraviolet radiation, heat, moisture, pollution, pests and abrasion. Label every claim as museum-textile evidence. Exclude figures, photos, vendor references, museum relative-humidity, temperature or light prescriptions, every cleaning, treatment, chemical, storage or emergency recipe, and any direct inference about jute or hemp bondage-rope strength, hygiene, conditioning, drying, inspection, retirement, body contact, uplines, anchors or human suspension.
rights_basis: Eligible NPS-authored federal text is public domain in the United States; preserve attribution and exclude photographs, figures, trademarks and separately credited components.

source_name: MAIB Report 2/2019: Failure of a throw-bag rescue line
discovered_from: hidden rope-construction defect and quality-control evidence gap
access_notes: The official GOV.UK landing page and eight-page MAIB Report 2/2019 are publicly accessible. The Crown-authored investigation found that a rescue line consisted of four rope segments joined by thermal fusion, that a hidden joint failed during a capsize drill, and that similar defects occurred across products or manufacturers; visual and random quality control did not reliably detect them. Product identities, test numbers, standards and credited photographs are not needed for the transferable process lesson.

representative_pages.note: Official landing page with report identity, event and publication context.
Complete eight-page Crown report with construction, failure, testing, quality-control analysis and separately credited photographs.
Open Government Licence version 3 terms for eligible Crown material.

decision_reason: The short official investigation provides unusually clear evidence that hidden construction defects can defeat visual inspection and random quality control. Its traceability and independent-verification lessons are valuable when product names, numbers and rescue-operation transfer are excluded.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create a four- to five-page-equivalent, text-only OGL digest of the government-authored failure and quality-control findings. Preserve the four-segment construction, hidden thermally fused joints, joint failure during a drill, occurrence of similar defects across products or manufacturers, large qualitative difference between intact rope and joints, visual-inspection and random-quality-control limitations, and the need for construction traceability, batch control and independent verification. Exclude all manufacturer and product identities, photographs and figures, standards or legal text, dimensions, loads, ratios and test values, material or joining recipes, rescue-use instructions, and every transfer to bondage rope, body contact, knots, anchors, uplines or human suspension.
rights_basis: Eligible Crown-authored report text is reusable under the Open Government Licence version 3; preserve attribution and changes, and exclude credited photographs, standards, trademarks and separately owned components.

source_name: Choking/Strangulation During Sex: Understanding and Negotiating ‘Safety’ Among 18–35 Year Old Australians
access_notes: The 2025 Archives of Sexual Behavior article is available under CC BY 4.0 through an official University of Melbourne repository bitstream whose robots policy permits the bitstream route. It analyzes 3,209 selected open-text responses from a 5,054-person Australian survey, but uses one final open question, includes only responses mentioning safety or risk, exposed 75 percent of respondents to a risk prompt immediately beforehand, and reports no coding-reliability measure. Participant beliefs about pressure, consent, trust and communication include dangerous technique claims and cannot become instruction.

representative_pages.note: Canonical publisher identity, DOI, abstract and CC BY license route; publisher body is not the selected capture endpoint.
Official, robots-permitted institutional bitstream containing the complete 13-page article, methods, selected qualitative findings and limitations.
Article-stated Attribution license.

decision_reason: The only safe training yield is the narrow epistemic lesson that consent, trust, intention and participant belief do not establish physical safety. The high-risk act, selected and primed responses, absent reliability measure and unsafe participant beliefs require an extremely small methods-and-misconceptions digest.
recommended_crawl_scope: Use only the official University of Melbourne CC BY bitstream and create less than one page-equivalent of methods, limitations and non-operational safety reasoning after specialist safety review. Preserve study title, authors, year, DOI, Australian 18-to-35 context, 5,054 survey participants, 3,209 safety-or-risk-selected responses, single final open question, 75-percent prior risk-prompt exposure, no reported coding-reliability measure, ethics identifier, funding and no-conflict statement. State only that consent, trust, intent, communication and participant belief are ethical or perceptual dimensions and cannot establish that a dangerous physical act is safe. Exclude all participant quotations, prevalence numbers beyond method denominators, exact injury or onset or death timing, pressure, intensity, duration, anatomy or technique beliefs, medical, first-aid, prognosis or legal advice, and anything usable as choking, strangulation, neck-rope or breath-play instruction.
rights_basis: The official institutional article states CC BY 4.0; preserve full attribution, DOI, license, change indication and methodological limits, and exclude participant quotations and separately credited components.

source_name: National Park Service maritime rope construction and ropewalk history
access_notes: Three short NPS pages provide federal-authored text on yarn-to-strand-to-rope hierarchy, spinning, forming, counter-twist or laying, historical hemp ropewalk production, and maritime line, cable and hawser-laid terminology. The pages are publicly accessible, but credited Boston Public Library, Naval History and Heritage Command and other images or external-source components remain separately owned. This is historical and terminological material, not modern strength or safety evidence.

representative_pages.note: Current NPS history of the Charlestown Navy Yard ropewalk, rope hierarchy and manufacturing context.
NPS maritime rope terminology and construction page.
NPS description of spinning, forming and counter-twist rope-making.

decision_reason: This compact public-domain bundle fills basic rope-construction and historical terminology gaps with authoritative prose. It must remain a text-only history digest and cannot support modern material selection, strength or rigging claims.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create a short, text-only public-domain digest from the three named NPS pages. Preserve yarn-to-strand-to-rope hierarchy, spinning, forming, laying and counter-twist concepts, clearly attributed historical hemp and ropewalk production, and source-labeled maritime distinctions among rope, line, cable and hawser-laid construction. Exclude every photograph and illustration unless independently cleared, Boston Public Library, NHHC and external-source wording, wartime production numbers and trivia, and all modern strength, care, retirement, tying, rigging, anchor, upline, body-contact or human-suspension claims.
rights_basis: Eligible NPS-authored federal prose is public domain in the United States; preserve attribution and exclude credited photographs, illustrations, external-source passages, trademarks and separately owned components.

source_name: Accuracy of Polyester Subrope Damage Detection by ROV Inspection and Assessment of Remaining Rope Strength and Life
access_notes: The official BSEE record and 117-page 2007 MMS contractor report are publicly accessible. Stress Engineering Services with TTI Ltd authored the work under MMS contract M07PX13203; agency review and approval do not make contractor text public domain, and no open reuse license was found. The report addresses permanently moored offshore polyester systems, ROV inspection, damage mechanisms, FMEA, measurement concepts, insert testing and remaining-life models rather than body rope.

representative_pages.note: Official research record, title, contractor, project identity and report route.
Complete 117-page contractor report with disclaimer, methods, figures, equations, models, vendor material and no open-content grant.

decision_reason: The qualitative inspection-limit and failure-mechanism material is unusually relevant, but contractor copyright remains unresolved and the offshore remaining-life body carries major numeric and cross-domain transfer risks. Permission is required before transformation.
recommended_crawl_scope: Retain title, date, report and contract identifiers, MMS or BSEE record, Stress Engineering Services and TTI authorship, 117-page extent, contractor-rights uncertainty, offshore polyester-subrope context, ROV visual or non-invasive inspection topic, qualitative damage-mechanism taxonomy, FMEA, insert testing, measurement-device and remaining-life-model topics, and permission leads as metadata only. Do not queue, copy, paraphrase, transform, translate, derive QA, or reproduce the body, equations, tables, photos, figures, vendor statements, standards, numeric ratings, thresholds, life formulas, API criteria or offshore procedures. If written commercial derivative and model-training permission is obtained, create only a qualitative methods-and-uncertainty digest; never transfer it to natural fiber, bondage rope, visual retirement, knots, body contact, anchors, uplines or human suspension.
rights_basis: The report was authored by Stress Engineering Services with TTI under a federal contract; agency review and public hosting do not establish public-domain status or a derivative-use license, so written rights-holder permission is required.

source_name: Shibari Lounge UK risk profile and accessibility policies
access_notes: Public journal and policy prose is readable and the footer states CC BY 4.0, but June 2025 terms grant only limited nontransferable noncommercial use, allow educational or community sharing with credit and no modifications, and reserve ungranted rights. source_name: FEMA RTLT Structural Collapse Search Technician

source_name: United States Coast Guard Cutter Seamanship Manual, COMDTINST M3120.9

source_name: Technical Skills Teaching to MD Students: A Blinded, Randomized Controlled Trial Investigating Video Assistance in the Education of the Single-Handed Knot Tie
discovered_from: video adjunct and physical-skill retention evidence gap
access_notes: The final 2024 Discover Education article at DOI 10.1007/s44217-024-00101-1 is CC BY 4.0. Twenty-nine knot-naive first-year medical students were randomized after all received the same 1.5-hour face-to-face workshop with demonstration, individualized tuition and feedback; the intervention additionally had optional video access. The assessor was blinded, but video use was not monitored and neither group was restricted from external content. Only 6 intervention and 8 control students returned at four weeks. The video group tied more knots initially and had better follow-up global quality, while the follow-up efficiency difference was weak and nonsignificant. The small sample, severe attrition, short follow-up and subjective global rating limit inference. The authors report no funding and no conflicts.

source_name: From Lab to Real Life: Is There a Link Between Lab-Based and Ecological Assessment of Procedural Perceptual-Motor Learning Tasks?
discovered_from: physical knot learning and ecological validity evidence gap
access_notes: The final 2025 PLOS ONE article at DOI 10.1371/journal.pone.0319715 is publicly readable under CC BY. Forty-two adults, including 31 women and 4 left-handed participants, completed two laboratory and six ecological tasks in one session. The knot task was one four-element motor-sequence task learned from first-person video across five blocks. Laboratory and ecological sequence-learning measures correlated only weakly to moderately, supporting partial rather than interchangeable ecological validity. Limits include the small gender-imbalanced sample, different measurement precision, fixed laboratory-task order, a single short session and no long-term retention. The authors report French foundation and ANR funding, no funder role and no conflicts.

representative_pages.note: Final PLOS HTML with physical knot task, methods, correlations, limitations, funding, conflicts and CC BY notice.
Persistent DOI identity.

decision_reason: CC BY evidence directly warns that a precise laboratory motor-sequence score is not a substitute for physical knot learning: correlations were only weak to moderate. The small single-session study does not validate any rope technique, teaching recipe or long-term learning claim.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create an attributed CC BY methods, finding-direction and limitations digest preserving DOI, 2025 date, 42 adults, 31 women, 4 left-handed participants, two laboratory and six ecological tasks, one-session design, first-person-video four-element knot task, five practice blocks, low-to-moderate lab-to-ecological correlations, funding, no funder role and no conflicts. Retain only that laboratory motor-sequence measures partially reflect but do not replace physical real-world skill assessment, and that knot learning may combine sequence acquisition with adaptation. Preserve measurement-precision mismatch, fixed laboratory-task order, small gender-imbalanced sample, short-delay interference block and absence of long-term retention. Exclude knot identity, video, instructions, scoring formulas, exact effects, figures, tables and supplements. Do not infer rope-bondage tying competence, security, load behavior, body contact, upline or human-suspension safety.
rights_basis: The final PLOS article is CC BY; preserve attribution, DOI, license and changes, and exclude video, figures, tables, supplements and separately credited components.

source_name: Laparoscopic Suturing Training Using Positive or Negative Feedback: A Randomized Controlled Trial
discovered_from: instructional feedback tone and learner-pressure evidence gap
access_notes: The final article at DOI 10.1007/s00464-025-12285-0 was published online in November 2025 and issued in 2026 under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0. Forty-two laparoscopic-naive medical students were randomized equally to encouraging, critical or no-feedback groups during a standardized two-hour simulation with one-week reassessment. The positive-feedback group showed more favorable motivation and pressure direction, while the critical-feedback group had more observed tissue injury. The small sample, single session and model, post-only motivation inventory, subjective assessment and absence of combined feedback limit transfer. The authors disclose two Chinese education-program funding sources and no conflicts.

Preserve ICAR, National Jute Board and Jute Corporation of India support, facilities acknowledgments, no-conflict declaration and no-funder-role statement. decision_reason: The paper offers unusually clear evidence that superficially similar slip-knot topologies can loosen or tighten under pull and that surface friction can help or hinder sliding depending on topology. Only this qualitative mechanism and its strict micro-scale limits merit conversion.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create a short attributed CC BY 4.0 evidence-and-limitations digest from the official article HTML. Preserve authors, DOI, 12 February 2016 date, raw versus chemically degummed single Bombyx mori silk-fiber context, two experimental slip-knot topologies, and the qualitative findings that one topology loosened while the other tightened under pull, sliding produced energy dissipation, surface friction changed that sliding, and extra friction could either support dissipation or promote damage and early failure depending on topology. Preserve funding and no-competing-financial-interest statements. Exclude every value, equation, optimization, dimension, sample-preparation or degumming recipe, apparatus, figure, table, image, supplementary file, named performance rank and operational knot instruction. A single silk fiber is not finished rope, the experimental knots are not established rope-bondage knots, and the paper supplies no security, release, care, strength, load, body-contact, upline, anchor, bondage or human-suspension rule.
rights_basis: The official article states CC BY 4.0; preserve authors, DOI, license, disclosures and changes, and exclude figures, supplementary material and separately credited components.

recommended_crawl_scope: Retain title, authors, DOI, 2024 date, conference and OJS identities, copyright notice, funding lead, named-community context, Q'oya or Festuca dolichophylla material terms, Q'eswa component-rope and Maqui or Duro architecture terms, permission need and cultural-authority questions as metadata only. Exclude exact construction recipes, every value, figure, table, rating, procurement detail and current performance or safety recommendation. A heritage bridge study does not validate a bondage rope, knot, body-contact method, working load, upline, anchor or human-suspension practice.

source_name: Mechanical characterization of banana peduncle fiber ropes
access_notes: The complete official seven-page BRIN journal PDF was manually reviewed and is CC BY-SA 4.0. It compares small sets of banana-peduncle, abaca and coir rope conditions, but material, production, treatment and market-source differences are confounded; apparatus and short-specimen limitations, specimen-code inconsistencies and a hazardous bleaching or boiling procedure materially limit safe reuse.

representative_pages.note: Official article record, DOI, abstract and CC BY-SA 4.0 journal context.
Official complete PDF reviewed for methods, specimen inconsistencies, limitations and chemical-treatment contamination.

decision_reason: Although openly licensed, this small comparative study has too many method, coding, treatment and material-manufacture confounds to enter training without textile-methods and chemical-safety review. Its hazardous treatment must never be reframed as rope-care advice.
recommended_crawl_scope: Retain citation, DOI, BRIN journal identity, CC BY-SA 4.0 status, tested material families, foot-spun versus marketed and bleached versus unbleached comparison labels, five-specimen condition, apparatus and short-specimen limitations, specimen-code inconsistencies, material or manufacture confounding and specialist-review need as metadata only. Before any conversion, obtain textile-testing review and chemical-safety review and verify whether ShareAlike is compatible with the target distribution. If accepted later, retain only a source-specific lesson that material label alone cannot separate manufacture and treatment effects. Exclude every value, figure, table, supplier claim, performance rating, bleaching or boiling recipe and outcome as care advice. Banana peduncle is not jute or hemp, and these ropes establish no hygiene, cleaning, body-contact, working-load, upline, anchor, bondage or human-suspension rule.
rights_basis: The article is CC BY-SA 4.0, but license availability does not cure method, safety or compatibility concerns; attribution, ShareAlike compatibility and component rights must be reviewed before transformation.

Preserve that capacity depends jointly on the support condition and connection geometry, the available structure constrains possible attachments, out-of-plumb force paths can add loads, and a qualified evaluator needs owner and structural information. Retain official eTool identity, shipyard hanging-staging domain and the distinction between identifying a location and qualifying it. The page does not qualify any tree, ceiling, beam, domestic structure, tension system, portable frame, bondage hardpoint, upline or human suspension; current site-specific structural and rigging evaluation controls.
rights_basis: Eligible DOL or OSHA employee-authored federal prose is public domain in the United States; VSRA photos, other third-party media, regulations and incorporated components are excluded.

decision_reason: The national resource supplies rights-clear, direct language for free, voluntary and informed consent, active communication, uncertainty, withdrawal, check-ins and separate digital-image permissions, with clear government provenance and component exclusions.
recommended_crawl_scope: Acquire manually and at low rate only after robots policy is resolved. Create an attributed text-only CC BY 4.0 digest of consent as free, voluntary and informed; active and communicated; ongoing and mutual; capacity-dependent; and specific to an activity. Retain regular check-ins, uncertainty signaled through body language or freezing, withdrawal, privacy, separate permission for recording or sharing images, and supportive responses to disclosure. Exclude all images and photographs, logos, trademarks, third-party resource bodies, legal or jurisdiction-specific claims, dated statistics, youth-specific examples, First Nations or translated components unless separately rights-audited, and every claim that this material certifies a rope scene, tie or participant.
rights_basis: DSS applies CC BY 4.0 to eligible Consent.gov.au text; the Coat of Arms, logos, trademarks, all images and photographs, third-party content and specifically excluded material remain outside scope.

source_name: NDIS Commission supported decision-making, intimacy and communication standards
discovered_from: disability autonomy intimacy and supported decision-making gap
access_notes: The public pages were manually accessible, although robots retrieval timed out and automated crawling must wait for policy resolution. The NDIS Commission copyright policy applies CC BY 4.0 Australia to eligible material while excluding the Coat of Arms, logo, third-party material and specifically identified components.

representative_pages.note: Official rights, communication, privacy, informed-choice, dignity-of-risk, intimacy and sexual-expression outcomes; updated 10 October 2024.
Official supported decision-making process in which decisions are made by the person and not for them; updated 29 June 2026.
Governing CC BY 4.0 Australia policy and exclusions.

decision_reason: The pages add disability-specific autonomy, intimacy and supported-decision principles with strong provenance: communication in the person's likely understood mode, sufficient time and tailored support, and decisions made by them and not for them.
recommended_crawl_scope: After robots policy is resolved, create an attributed CC BY 4.0 Australia digest of communication in the language, mode of communication and terms a person is most likely to understand; privacy and agreement about personal information or recordings; informed choice, dignity of risk, sufficient time and advocates; autonomy including the right to intimacy and sexual expression; will and preferences; tailored decision tools; trusted supporters; provider conflicts; and decisions made by them and not for them. Preserve official update dates and the NDIS service context. Do not infer decisional incapacity from disability, substitute supporter or provider preference for the person's choice, or transfer NDIS legal duties to general rope consent. Exclude legal and regulatory detail, examples, images, logos, third-party Flinders resources, and any claim that support proves understanding, consent, competence or scene safety.
rights_basis: Eligible NDIS Commission material is available under CC BY 4.0 Australia; the Coat of Arms, Commission logo, third-party material and specifically excluded components remain outside scope.

source_name: Australian eSafety consent and intimate-image privacy guidance
discovered_from: recording screenshot and onward-sharing consent gap
access_notes: The public pages were manually accessible, although robots retrieval timed out and automated crawling must wait for policy resolution. eSafety's policy defaults eligible website content to CC BY 4.0 while excluding the Coat of Arms, eSafety logo, trademarks, third-party content, all videos, images and songs, the Who we are section and eSafety Guide.

representative_pages.note: Official active, specific, ongoing and withdrawable consent guidance; last updated 5 December 2024.
Official distinction among creation, possession and onward sharing of intimate images.
Governing CC BY 4.0 default and detailed component exclusions; updated 20 March 2025.

decision_reason: The source adds a rights-clear separation between consent to activity, recording or screenshot creation, possession and onward sharing, along with check-ins and nonverbal uncertainty signals.
recommended_crawl_scope: After robots policy is resolved, create an attributed text-only CC BY 4.0 digest of informed and freely given active consent, regular check-ins, uncertainty or nonverbal discomfort, specificity, withdrawal, and distinct permission for each recording or screenshot and each sharing action. Preserve that permission to share with one person does not authorize onward sharing. Retain supportive, non-blaming responses after a privacy violation. Exclude all images, videos and songs, logos, trademarks, third-party research or quotations, the eSafety Guide and Who we are section, legal or jurisdiction-specific and age claims, platform or reporting-tool instructions, medical claims, statistics and any claim that the source certifies a rope scene.
rights_basis: Eligible eSafety website text defaults to CC BY 4.0; the Coat of Arms, eSafety logo, trademarks, third-party content, all videos, images and songs, the Who we are section and eSafety Guide are excluded.

source_name: UK Government working definition of trauma-informed practice
discovered_from: trauma-aware teaching and scene-management boundary gap
access_notes: The official GOV.UK HTML page is public, its page-level notice applies the Open Government Licence v3.0 to eligible Crown copyright 2022 text, and GOV.UK robots permits the content path while disallowing print and search routes. Third-party material remains excluded.

representative_pages.note: Official OGL working definition and six principles.
GOV.UK Crown copyright, OGL v3.0 and third-party exclusions.

decision_reason: A small OGL digest can add a clearly bounded framework of safety, trust, choice, collaboration, empowerment and cultural consideration while distinguishing trauma-informed conduct from trauma-specialist treatment.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create a short attributed OGL v3.0 digest of the six stated principles: safety, trust, choice, collaboration, empowerment and cultural consideration. Retain preventing re-traumatization, asking what a person needs, transparent expectations and the distinction between trauma-informed conduct and trauma-specialist treatment. Preserve the health and care source domain. Exclude cited third-party definition bodies, clinical claims, diagnosis, screening, treatment or therapy instructions, claims about individual trauma histories and every claim that this framework certifies consent or safety. Any rope-scene application is an inference and must be labeled as such; this is not trauma treatment.
rights_basis: The page applies OGL v3.0 to eligible Crown copyright 2022 text; third-party material and separately identified rights are excluded.

source_name: UK Government disability, loneliness and relationships thematic report
discovered_from: disabled people's relationship autonomy and support gap
access_notes: The official GOV.UK HTML page is public, explicitly applies OGL v3.0 to eligible Crown copyright 2025 material and is permitted by GOV.UK robots. The report includes extensive third-party quotations and citations that remain excluded.

representative_pages.note: Official thematic synthesis, methodology and limitations based on 100 analyzed qualitative records from a 158-record family and private-sphere sample.
GOV.UK Crown copyright, OGL v3.0 and third-party exclusions.

decision_reason: The report supplies an unusually direct government-authored synthesis of disabled people's relationship autonomy and barriers, including assumed asexuality, infantilization, overprotection, privacy, alternative communication and intersecting stigma, while documenting serious sample limits.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create an attributed OGL v3.0 digest using only government-authored synthesis, methods and limitations. Preserve the 'nothing about us without us' framing; reported barriers of assumed asexuality or incompetence and infantilization; autonomy versus overprotection; support without substitution; privacy; intersecting LGBTQ+ and disability stigma; exclusion through inaccessible or alternative communication; accessible sex and relationship education; and power differentials involving carers or support networks. Preserve that 158 qualitative family or private-sphere records were screened, 58 excluded and 100 thematically analyzed, along with small-sample, limited-peer-review and underrepresentation limits. Exclude every long third-party quotation, cited-source body, statistic beyond method identity, UK legal claim, clinical claim and unsupported generalization. Do not infer incapacity from disability or transfer a population theme to an individual.
rights_basis: The page explicitly applies OGL v3.0 to eligible Crown copyright 2025 material; quotations, cited-source bodies and every third-party component remain excluded.

source_name: UK Government accessible communication formats guidance
discovered_from: accessible consent and safety communication authoring gap
access_notes: The official GOV.UK HTML page is public, explicitly applies OGL v3.0 to eligible Crown copyright 2026 material and is permitted by GOV.UK robots. Third-party standards, symbols, trademarks, examples and media remain excluded.

representative_pages.note: Official accessible-format authoring guidance updated 17 June 2026.
GOV.UK Crown copyright, OGL v3.0 and third-party exclusions.

decision_reason: The OGL page provides compact authoring guidance for offering content in audience-appropriate formats, including co-design, HTML, plain language, Easy Read, captions, audio and other alternatives, with a useful quality-assurance principle.
recommended_crawl_scope: Create an attributed OGL v3.0 digest for dataset, education, consent and safety communication authoring. Retain co-design with Deaf and disabled people; HTML where possible; plain language without jargon; concise audience-specific content; accessible formats alongside standard content; that Easy Read is not the same as plain language; summaries that do not withhold detailed information; audio, BSL, captions, braille, large print, SMS or web chat alternatives; and quality assurance by people who understand the relevant disability. Exclude legal requirements, statistics, images, examples, third-party standards, vendor details, Makaton symbols and trademarks, and every claim that a format proves understanding or consent.
rights_basis: The page explicitly applies OGL v3.0 to eligible Crown copyright 2026 material; third-party standards, symbols, trademarks, examples and media remain excluded.

source_name: National Diet Library digitization of Ito Seiu's Seme no Hanashi (1929)
discovered_from: primary Japanese kinbaku history and terminology gap
access_notes: Official NDL metadata and the IIIF manifest identify the exact 1929 work, author, publisher and PDM status, and the Digital Collections record is internet-public. NDL reuse guidance says protection-period-expired works may be reused without contacting NDL, with attribution and frame-level checks. Territorial, component, moral-rights, human-rights and privacy review still applies; no image or frame was captured.

representative_pages.note: Official bibliographic record for 責の話 by 伊藤晴雨, published by 温故書屋 in 1929, 182 pages, with PDM rights metadata.
Official NDL Digital Collections work identity and public-access record; images are excluded from discovery and conversion scope.
Official manifest confirming title, author, publisher, publication date, PDM status and NDL attribution.
Official NDL reuse guidance for protection-period-expired and component-sensitive material.

decision_reason: This exact work resolves one public-domain item from the earlier broad Ito Seiu lead and can add primary historical and terminology evidence, but only through expert Japanese review, work-level OCR verification and source-critical framing that excludes operational coercive content.
recommended_crawl_scope: Acquire the exact 1929 edition only, preserve title 責の話, transliteration Seme no Hanashi, author 伊藤晴雨, publisher 温故書屋, NDL record and persistent identifier, and credit the National Diet Library. Require a Japanese-language historical reviewer, frame-level component and territorial audit, work-level OCR correction and translation review. Retain only clearly attributable first-person historical, artistic, terminology and date context, always labeled as a primary source rather than neutral modern history. Exclude all images, later editorial matter, OCR guesses, operational restraint or torture technique, unsafe procedures, and modern consent or safety norms. Make no direct hojojutsu-to-kinbaku lineage inference and no inventor, father or universal-authority claim.
rights_basis: The exact NDL record and IIIF manifest mark the 1929 work PDM, and NDL reuse guidance permits reuse of protection-period-expired items with attribution and component checks; conversion remains contingent on territorial, frame-level, moral-rights, human-rights and privacy review, with all images excluded.

source_name: NSW Make No Doubt consent page
access_notes: The public page is permitted by the site's generic robots policy. Although NSW site content generally defaults to CC BY 4.0, this page carries a specific third-party copyright notice allowing social sharing while requiring permission for other uses; the page-specific notice controls.

representative_pages.note: Official page with a specific third-party copyright limitation and permission contact.
General NSW CC BY 4.0 policy, which does not override the page-specific restriction.

decision_reason: The page has useful direct-check-in and nonverbal-warning language, but its explicit third party copyright limitations permit social-media sharing only and require permission for all other reuse; rights-clear federal sources cover most concepts.
recommended_crawl_scope: Retain title, canonical URL, NSW provenance, the page-specific third party copyright limitations, social-media-only sharing allowance, permission requirement and sexualviolence@facs.nsw.gov.au contact as metadata only. Do not retrieve, copy, paraphrase or store the page or PDF body for training under the default site CC BY policy. If written derivative commercial model-training permission is obtained from all relevant rightsholders, separately review direct asking, check-ins, freezing, tensing, moving away, specificity, continuation and withdrawal, excluding images, videos, campaign copy, legal or age claims, statistics and any claim that the page certifies consent or scene safety.
rights_basis: A page-specific notice states that the material is subject to third party copyright limitations, permits social-media sharing and requires permission for other uses; it overrides the default NSW website CC BY 4.0 policy.

source_name: Better Health Channel disability and sexuality pages
discovered_from: disability sexuality education and support gap
access_notes: The public pages are permitted by robots with crawl-delay 2, but the official terms prohibit adaptation, reproduction, storage, derivative works, distribution, publication and commercialisation without written permission. Public access is not corpus permission.

representative_pages.note: Official health-information page, reviewed 23 April 2026; body remains excluded pending permission.
Related official page; body remains excluded pending permission and specialist review.
Official terms explicitly restricting adaptation, reproduction, storage, derivative works and commercialisation.

decision_reason: The pages are useful permission leads for disability sexual autonomy, privacy, accessible education and individualized support, but their terms expressly prohibit the storage and derivative transformation required for a corpus.
recommended_crawl_scope: Retain titles, canonical URLs, review date, Victorian health-department provenance, robots crawl-delay and the terms language prohibiting adapt, reproduce, store, derivative works, distribution, publication and commercialisation as metadata only. Obtain written permission for derivative commercial model training before any body capture. If cleared, require disability, medical and language review; preserve autonomy, privacy, individualized communication and education support while excluding medical or legal advice, dated or unsupported claims, diagnostic generalizations, problematic phrasing, images, referrals and any inference that disability establishes incapacity or that a page certifies a scene.
rights_basis: Better Health Channel terms expressly prohibit adaptation, reproduction, storage, derivative works, distribution, publication and commercialisation without written permission; private-study exceptions do not authorize a training corpus.

source_name: National Diet Library record for Torinawajutsu Osso Kanshi Kenkyu no Shiori (1921)
access_notes: The official NDL record is internet-public, but its rights metadata is undetermined and publication occurred through a Commissioner for Cultural Affairs arbitration under Copyright Act article 67(1). NDL reuse guidance says Internet publication by arbitration remains copyright-protected and requires rightsholder permission.

representative_pages.note: Official bibliographic and rights-status record for 捕縄術押送監視研究之栞 by 野村昌靖, published by 瑞岳堂 in 1921.
Official internet-public work identity; body and images remain excluded.
Official guidance that internet publication by arbitration remains protected and requires copyright-holder permission.

decision_reason: The exact 1921 record is a potentially valuable institutional hojojutsu history lead, but undetermined rights and internet publication by arbitration leave it copyright-protected, while its operational coercive-restraint subject requires exceptional harm review.
recommended_crawl_scope: Retain bibliographic metadata, canonical NDL URLs, author 野村昌靖, publisher 瑞岳堂, 1921 date, 37-page extent, undetermined-rights status, Commissioner for Cultural Affairs arbitration date and permission requirement as metadata only. Do not retrieve, OCR, translate, summarize or store body text or images. If every rightsholder grants derivative commercial model-training permission, require Japanese historical and harm review and retain only source-critical institutional vocabulary and context. Exclude all actionable restraint or torture technique, atemi, transport, surveillance or control procedure, safety or consent transfer, images, OCR guesses and any direct lineage or modern-practice claim.
rights_basis: NDL marks the work's copyright status undetermined and identifies internet publication by Commissioner for Cultural Affairs arbitration; NDL reuse guidance says such material remains protected and requires copyright-holder permission.
